title: Evangelion without Shinji as the Third Children
display_title: Evangelion without Shinji as the Third Children
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion without Shinji as the Third Children             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 04, 2014 10:44 am Reposting the idea from this post . It's not a new idea, but it's plainly something worth discussing. What if Shinji was never supposed to be the Third Children? Rei was the first, obvious choice for Gendo as an Evangelion pilot, and Asuka became the second choice after her mother's contact experiment. He didn't want to bring Shinji into this (he was staying away from Shinji because he was afraid that he would only harm him) until circumstances surrounding Sachiel's arrival forced his hand (Rei being injured and incapable of fighting, Asuka being away in Germany with Eva-02). So Shinji became the third, and it had to be Shinji with Yui's soul in Eva-01 taking care of things. That does raise the question of whether Asuka and Rei would have been able to hold Tokyo-3 if Asuka and Eva-02 had been available in time for Sachiel. Toji might have become the third assuming that Eva-03 was transferred over without getting infected with Bardiel, and Shinji and Eva-01 may not even have come into the picture until the very end (Rei can synch with Eva-01, as seen in Episode 14, and the Dummy System was coming into being). Aside from the instances like the Matarael and Sahaquiel battles where all three pilots sortie together and assuming that Gendo could lay his hands on Eva-03 without getting the Eva infected by Bardiel, it seems fairly plausible that he could run the entire show without Shinji. Sure, Yui going berserk is incredibly useful, but I have the impression that Gendo didn't really need Shinji to do any of that dirtywork except in the absence of replacements. Of course, the show wouldn't be Evangelion without Shinji, but I really think it's possible Gendo meant to do entirely without Shinji. Unlike Toji and all the pilot candidates in Tokyo-3, Shinji had no idea that Nerv, or anything like the Evangelions ever existed.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 04, 2014 12:04 pm I thought that was pretty obvious. Even Fuyu did not know that they did, in fact, have an available pilot (he complains about it when Sachiel arrives) - it was all a last Minute thing because Rei was still injured. Although Yui obviously DID intend for Shinji to pilot it. Kind of sparks the possibility that Yui didn't tell Gendo anything because as he was before her death, he would have massively objected to using Shinji for this. At least ep 20 suggests that he did once value the idea of his kid having a normal life. Rebuild is, of course, a wholly different matter. - On the one hand, it's painfully clear that Gendo planned everything Shinji winds up doing over the course of the movies from the very beginning, on the other, it seems like Yui was, at least partially , in on that plan ...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 04, 2014 12:16 pm Well, assuming all the Evangelions are finished at the same moment in your theory than in the series, without Shinji Ramiel wins and start Third Impact, since they needed a pilot to use the positron rifle rifle and another to shield him. Or they call Shinji in urgency to pilot EVA-01 and use the rifle while Rei shields him, and due to his complete inexperience he probably will be completely unable to do that, meaning that Ramiel will win. Assuming that they still manage to kill Ramiel with one pilot(shield bolted on the ground), it will be very difficult to catch Sahaquiel with only two pilots, since three at minimum are required to cover all of Tokyo-3's ground. Assuming that they got lucky and manage to catch Sahaquiel and kill it, they have their chances against Leliel : if it's Asuka who get absorbed, in which case Kyoko will surely go berserk and rip it apart like Yui in NGE, if it's Rei, they dump the thousand N² missiles, retrieve EVA-01 and the carbonized Rei is cloned. Assuming the best case scenario happens and EVA-03's transfer goes smoothly and that EVA-04 don't blow up nor is possessed by Bardiel, meaning that NERV ends with the enviable position of commanding four Evangelions, two of which piloted by trained pilots(Rei + Asuka) and two by complete rookies(Toji + Kensuke? Hikari?), and that the Dummy System is completed without Yui making a fuss... they still all dies when Zeruel attack, since nothing short of a Berserk Eva will defeat it, unless for some reason EVA--02 goes berserk and is as powerful as EVA-01. When you look at it, Rei's accident during EVA-00's activation test was a blessing from the gods, because without Shinji there wouldn't be berserk Yui against Zeruel, and NERV would be on pilot short against two Angels(Ramiel and Sahaquiel)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 04, 2014 12:26 pm Although they might have flown in Asuka earlier/faster. Given by how relatively fast they transport EVA 03, it seems like the whole slow ships and the huge fleet around them/all the troublesome preparations were just a cover for transporting Adam, which Gendo may have done/covered differently.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 04, 2014 12:53 pm Yeah, that was pretty much how I saw it. Shinji was never meant to be part of the plan but Gendo needed him so there he is. No Shinji = Sachiel wins.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Tue Feb 04, 2014 3:13 pm Don't know if this really belongs in this thread, but I was just considering something. At least Gendo (and probably several others, like Fuyutsuki and Ritsuko) knew Yui's soul was in Unit-01. And its pretty well-known that the mother-child piloting connection was the best way to pilot an EVA. But what if the idea was to bypass this with Rei? Perhaps they intended to use Rei initially as the pilot of Unit-01, thinking that since she was a clone of Yui, it would be enough to fulfill the mother-child connection. In the manga, Rei is shown to pilot Unit-01 before Shinji gets there, but what if in the show, Rei just couldn't sync with Unit-01 at all? Which would be why they decided to try out Unit-00 (this may have even been the point where they divided up Rei's soul, depending on when in the timelines this all occurred.) Then you have the Unit-00 activation, leaving that EVA suspended, and Rei injured. Then it would make sense why they would call Shinji, otherwise they would have at least tried to use Rei as a pilot. Remember, they weren't even looking for someone to pilot it, they were pretty much banking on it going berserk. Heck, another possibility is that Rei could sync with Unit-01, but wouldn't be enough for it to go into berserk mode to protect her. So, the real point is, if Rei was a compatible pilot for Unit-01, then Shinji wouldn't have even been brought in at all, and Unit-00 would have been kept on perma-freeze. Whether or not Rei could have taken Angels 3-5 on her own is another matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 04, 2014 5:23 pm It's likely that Gendo would have just sent Rei out, injured as she was. It's not like she wasn't replaceable.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Feb 04, 2014 6:30 pm I still maintain that the Evas were meant to be pilotless after they discovered the problem with adults and the kids were the only available option when they couldn't get it to work until it was almost too late. I doubt the city would have survived without Shinji and his mom since there are at least two instances where it was really Yui that pulled everyone's fat from the fryer and an automated system probably would have been trashed anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 04, 2014 6:44 pm Kendrix wrote: Although they might have flown in Asuka earlier/faster. Given by how relatively fast they transport EVA 03, it seems like the whole slow ships and the huge fleet around them/all the troublesome preparations were just a cover for transporting Adam, which Gendo may have done/covered differently.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 05, 2014 12:43 pm I accidently deleted this post earlier. Apparently it's been too long since I noticed so I can't save it from the abyss, but I can repost the contents. My bad. SoryuUberAlles wrote: Given what we know about the Vatican treaty, and the whole Jet Alone fiasco, I think there's a lot of politics behind Eva deployment and they don't just roll them off the production line with the cool paint jobs still glistening. 'Mans greatest enemy is man himself', after all. Sachiel advances? Nuke it like a dancing angel, airdrop Asuka. Specifically, I tend to think of Shinji's role in Gendou's plan as 'back-up Rei'. He might get an instrumentality out of those two, but Asuka would be totally unreliable at the moment of truth.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 06, 2014 4:26 am ^ Wait SoryuUberAlles, are you talking about NGE!Shinji or Rebuild!Shinji? Because in the Rebuild Shinji was planned as the pilot for EVA-01 since the very beginning, and was deliberately left in the dark and without any training by Gendo to make him easily manipulable into awakening EVA-01 with Rei.(hell it's even possible that abandoning him under the care of a stranger was already part of the plan!) It's in NGE that Shinji becoming the Third Child wasn't planned at all and only happened because NERV was backed against a corner due to Rei's injury and the Angels starting the war.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Thu Feb 06, 2014 5:50 pm ElMariachi wrote: ^ Wait SoryuUberAlles, are you talking about NGE!Shinji or Rebuild!Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 06, 2014 6:30 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: Just to prove I'm not a complete idiot: The way Ritsuko talks about the explosion of unit-04 in the manga implies that it's partially a political decision. Even then, there's some kind of bureaucratic will involved, in shifting EVAs around at the last moment, I'm pretty sure of that.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Feb 06, 2014 6:46 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: Was that why my post was deleted?

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Thu Feb 06, 2014 7:28 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Pure cock-up. It started with an accidental edit when a quote was intended and then things went downhill after that.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Feb 07, 2014 3:28 am SoryuUberAlles wrote: I'll always remember when Gendo says "The scrolls don't tell the whole story. It'll be a good lesson for the old men." One of his coolest lines in the manga. Looking up Ritsuko and Misato's conversation, though, it implies there's bargaining going on behind the scenes about the distribution of vital resources, and the loss of Unit-04 leads to the moving of Unit-03.

[]:             Quote Post by Nepheliad » Mon Jun 23, 2014 5:39 pm Hope y'all don't mind if I bump this old thread, but I love counterfactuals and wrote up a sort of play-by-play. A few things first - I think that on the whole, without Shinji, Asuka would be less distracted by sexual tension. However, I think whatever benefit that may give her would be at least somewhat counterbalanced by increased loneliness and being unnerved by Rei. Having to work in close proximity with Rei would be a very different dynamic, and however tense and painful her interactions with Shinji are, it is still a relationship she finds value in. IMO, Shinji's presence also actually prevents her from taking her earlier losses as hard, because there's always someone to save her (less responsibility and resultant stress, not that she would consciously like being saved) and because there is often someone else to blame ("baka Shinji", also related to responsibility). 1) The show would end at Sachiel assuming 02 was not rushed in production/rushed to Tokyo-3. If 02 was rushed in, Asuka would likely be able to handle it on her own, given Sachiel's relative lack of power. I will keep up this rushed-in Asuka assumption, because there's nothing interesting to talk about otherwise. 2) I don't think Asuka would have had more trouble than Shinji with Shamshel. That should be fine. 3) I think Ramiel is where things could start getting interesting. I don't think there'd be a difference in the first sortie (things happened too fast), so 02 would be blasted by Ramiel similarly to 01. I don't know how Asuka would handle it; I expect less overt fear, more overt rage at "losing". When Asuka loses to Israfel, she had Shinji to shuttle this blame onto; I guess she could move the blame to Misato, but if she doesn't, her mental stability takes a minor hit. Given the "cheapness" of Ramiel's shot, I'm not sure how much offense Asuka would take, though I find it likely she would feel vaguely insulted at having to to be protected by 00. Nevertheless, I don't see why 00 and 02 wouldn't successfully defeat Ramiel. 4) Asuka already solo'd Gaghiel, so this is a non-issue. 5) Israfel is another interesting case. I dunno how Asuka would react to having to work with Rei in such close proximity, given how much she resents puppets, and the fact that Rei would almost certainly be found to only be working with her because "they told her to". Assuming the positive, she would influence Rei to start thinking a little for herself through constant tirades. Assuming the neutral, her desire to defeat Israfel and avenge her loss would be strong enough that she would overlook any interpersonal issues she has with Rei; alternatively, Rei may just be incredibly damn good at synchronizing because she follows the lead of others very well. Assuming the negative, they may not be able to synchronize their dances enough to take Israfel down. I think the chances for success in some way are high, however. The conversation in episode 11 (about being Rei being "favored") would probably happen here, now that I think about it; given more time together in less urgent situations, I wonder what would have come of it...? 6) I don't think it matters whether Shinji or Rei threw down the prog knife. Would she remember thermal expansion without the requisite conversations with Shinji? Eh, probably. She's kind of in a mess of magma, and the heat alone would probably jog memories. Pretty sure Rei would rescue her in Shinji's place (don't think command can afford losing one of only two pilots). I think victory would be likely. 7) Three Evangelions were used, but I'm not sure whether three Evangelions were actually needed for Matarael. If Asuka could last a second or two longer on the defense, I don't think the time difference between passing the gun up and the gun retriever personally firing would matter. It didn't seem like Asuka was about to fail on defense up there, so I think it's pretty likely Rei could've fired the necessary shots to take down Matarael. 8) No increasing Shinji scores = more "stable" Asuka. Indeed, not having Shinji to drag around with her may mean increased effort in attempts to drag Rei around, but whether that would be either successful or helpful for both parties is hard for me to predict. If I had to guess, it would be more successful, but I'm not sure if Asuka would think much of the results - always having to drag a "friend" out makes one feel very unliked, after all, and would probably foster some resentment. This is a relatively tough battle for the pair, and it would benefit them if 03 arrived earlier at this point. I'm going to assume it doesn't, though, for simplicity's sake. The battle is immediately more difficult, as there are only two Evangelions to position in the range of Sahaquiel's landing zone. Assuming either Asuka or Rei make it in time, one would take up position holding Sahaquiel back; the other must both break through the AT field and stab the core. Watching the episode, it doesn't seem like an impossible task; after Rei breaks through the field, she's just holding it open, so perhaps she could get a stab in before the field closes. However, it would certainly be much, much tougher a task, also because there would be one less AT field among the Evangelions; the window of opportunity would be very brief in comparison. IMO, this is the first time Asuka and Rei are straight up likely to fail in comparison to having the full trio. Obviously, in Rebuild, it's just straight up impossible. I'll consider the rest of the angels later, since this is already pretty long, haha. I'm not sure it makes sense, but it was fun to consider.

Assuming the neutral, her desire to defeat Israfel and avenge her loss would be strong enough that she would overlook any interpersonal issues she has with Rei; alternatively, Rei may just be incredibly

Article: Evangelion without Shinji as the Third Children
 start thinking a little for herself through constant tirades. Assuming the neutral, her desire to defeat Israfel and avenge her loss would be strong enough that she would overlook any interpersonal issues she has with Rei; alternatively, Rei may just be incredibly damn good at synchronizing because she follows the lead of others very well. Assuming the negative, they may not be able to synchronize their dances enough to take Israfel down. I think the chances for success in some way are high, however. The conversation in episode 11 (about being Rei being "favored") would probably happen here, now that I think about it; given more time together in less urgent situations, I wonder what would have come of it...? 6) I don't think it matters whether Shinji or Rei threw down the prog knife. Would she remember thermal expansion without the requisite conversations with Shinji? Eh, probably. She's kind of in a mess of magma, and the heat alone would probably jog memories. Pretty sure Rei would rescue her in Shinji's place (don't think command can afford losing one of only two pilots). I think victory would be likely. 7) Three Evangelions were used, but I'm not sure whether three Evangelions were actually needed for Matarael. If Asuka could last a second or two longer on the defense, I don't think the time difference between passing the gun up and the gun retriever personally firing would matter. It didn't seem like Asuka was about to fail on defense up there, so I think it's pretty likely Rei could've fired the necessary shots to take down Matarael. 8) No increasing Shinji scores = more "stable" Asuka. Indeed, not having Shinji to drag around with her may mean increased effort in attempts to drag Rei around, but whether that would be either successful or helpful for both parties is hard for me to predict. If I had to guess, it would be more successful, but I'm not sure if Asuka would think much of the results - always having to drag a "friend" out makes one feel very unliked, after all, and would probably foster some resentment. This is a relatively tough battle for the pair, and it would benefit them if 03 arrived earlier at this point. I'm going to assume it doesn't, though, for simplicity's sake. The battle is immediately more difficult, as there are only two Evangelions to position in the range of Sahaquiel's landing zone. Assuming

Article: Evangelion without Shinji as the Third Children
 earlier at this point. I'm going to assume it doesn't, though, for simplicity's sake. The battle is immediately more difficult, as there are only two Evangelions to position in the range of Sahaquiel's landing zone. Assuming either Asuka or Rei make it in time, one would take up position holding Sahaquiel back; the other must both break through the AT field and stab the core. Watching the episode, it doesn't seem like an impossible task; after Rei breaks through the field, she's just holding it open, so perhaps she could get a stab in before the field closes. However, it would certainly be much, much tougher a task, also because there would be one less AT field among the Evangelions; the window of opportunity would be very brief in comparison. IMO, this is the first time Asuka and Rei are straight up likely to fail in comparison to having the full trio. Obviously, in Rebuild, it's just straight up impossible. I'll consider the rest of the angels later, since this is already pretty long, haha. I'm not sure it makes sense, but it was fun to consider.
